fields.label: EE SKU
fields.description: EasyEcom SKU — EE's unique product identifier per its FAQ; the join key for this row. Direction-agnostic: the row exists whether the product was born in EE (pulled in) or in ERPNext (pushed out). UNIQUE at the DB level (§8.1.2).

fields.label: Status
fields.description: §8.1.9 review/exception state. Mapped = clean; Created-Flagged = created but with a content problem (missing HSN, unmapped tax, dirty UOM); Flagged-Not-Created = unsupported type (variant_parent / child_product / kit_bom / unknown); Drift = (post-flip) unexpected EE-side change or EE-origin product; Disabled = lifecycle-deactivated. The status drives the FDE worklist filter.

fields.label: ERPNext DocType
fields.description: Either 'Item' (for a normal_product) or 'Product Bundle' (for a combo_product). Restricted to those two via the form-side query (see easyecom_item_map.js); the controller's validate refuses anything else. Dynamic-Link pattern: the doctype controls the options for erpnext_name.

fields.label: ERPNext Name
fields.description: The Item docname (for normal SKUs) OR the Product Bundle docname (for combo SKUs). Dynamic Link resolves the FK target from erpnext_doctype. The pull/push flows write here when they create the corresponding ERPNext-side row.

fields.label: EasyEcom Internal IDs
fields.description: EE's internal product_id and cp_id. Stored because the push functions (UpdateMasterProduct, ActivateDeactivateProduct) key on product_id; NOT used as the join key (sku is). Populated when the push creates a new product (CreateMasterProduct returns product_id) or when the pull discovers an existing one.

fields.label: EE product_id
fields.description: EasyEcom internal product id (returned by CreateMasterProduct; used by Update/ActivateDeactivate). Read-only after creation in normal flow; FDE can edit if remapping.

fields.label: EE cp_id
fields.description: EasyEcom internal cp_id (per-company product id; surfaced in some EE responses). Stored for completeness; not the join key.

fields.label: Flag Reason
fields.description: For Created-Flagged / Flagged-Not-Created / Drift rows: a plain-English explanation the FDE can act on (e.g. 'missing HSN', 'unsupported product_type=variant_parent', 'EE-side edit to product_name after onboarding flip'). For Drift rows, the structured per-field diff lives in the Drift Fields table below; flag_reason summarises (e.g. '3 fields drifted').

fields.label: Re-evaluate from EE
fields.description: Re-pull THIS SKU from EE and re-run the flag-evaluation, without triggering the account-wide cursor walk. Use after you've fixed the source of a flag (set tax_rule_name in EE, corrected the UOM, etc.) and want to verify this row flips back to Mapped immediately. Walks GetProductMaster up to ~200 pages looking for the SKU — fast on small catalogues, ~5–10s on a 2000-product one. If the SKU is no longer in EE (deleted upstream), the call surfaces that cleanly.

fields.label: Mark Mapped (override flags)
fields.description: FDE/SM override — flip a Created-Flagged row to Mapped WITHOUT fixing the underlying flag(s). Use case: the flag is genuinely benign for this Item (e.g. EE doesn't carry a tax_rule_name for a non-taxable accessory and the FDE has confirmed that's intentional) and the FDE wants to suppress it without changing source data. The next pull WILL re-evaluate — if the same flag fires again, the row flips back to Created-Flagged. To suppress permanently, fix the source (recommended) or add field-level exclusions if/when that becomes a supported pattern for CF rows. Each override is audit-logged via Frappe's Comment trail. Confirm dialog quotes the current flag_reason so the FDE acknowledges what they're suppressing.

fields.label: Drift Details
fields.description: Populated by the §8d post-flip drift detector (§8.1.8). One row per ERPNext field where the incoming EE payload differs from the current ERPNext doc. Cleared when the FDE dismisses (status → Mapped) or pushes ERPNext to EE (the next pull finds no drift).

fields.label: Drift Fields
fields.description: Per-field divergence. field=ERPNext field name, erpnext_value=current ERPNext value, ee_value=what EE asserts (NOT accepted). Structured replacement for the prior `||`-delimited Data flag_reason; sortable / filterable in the desk.

fields.label: Dismiss Drift (Mark Reviewed)
fields.description: FDE: the EE-side change is wrong / already-handled-upstream. Returns the row to Mapped and clears the Drift table; the underlying ERPNext doc is NOT touched. The next pull will re-detect if the EE-side state still differs (use the exclude list below to silence persistent intentional divergence).

fields.label: Push ERPNext → EE (Re-assert SoT)
fields.description: FDE: ERPNext is the source of truth (per the post-flip contract); push current ERPNext values to EE via the §8d push, overwriting the EE-side divergence. Dispatches to push_one_product (or push_one_bundle for combos).

fields.label: Drift Exclusions
fields.description: Fields the drift detector should IGNORE on this Item. Use case: FDE intentionally renames item_name in ERPNext (e.g. cleaner internal name vs EE marketplace title) and doesn't want every nightly pull to re-flag it. Empty list = compare every field (default Stage-5 behaviour).

fields.label: Push Snapshot
fields.description: Last successful push payload, used to diff against current ERPNext state so the NEXT update sends only the changed fields (per EE contract: with productId, send only the field(s) being updated). Refreshed automatically on every successful push.
